title: FLF: Cloud Service Exfiltration Hunt
description: FreeLabFriday-inspired hunt for suspicious non-browser processes uploading or connecting to trusted cloud services such as Microsoft Graph, OneDrive, Google Drive, Dropbox, and Slack.
query: ('Log Source' = 'SOC Sysmon Network Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs') and 'Event ID' = '3' and ('Destination Hostname' like '*graph.microsoft.com*' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*login.microsoftonline.com*' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*drive.google.com*' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*dropbox.com*' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*slack.com*' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*onedrive*') and not (('Process Name' like '*\\OneDrive.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\Teams.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\OUTLOOK.EXE' or 'Process Name' like '*\\msedge.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\chrome.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\firefox.exe')) | stats count as CloudApiEvents, unique('Destination Hostname') as CloudHosts by 'Host Name (Server)', 'Process Name', 'Destination IP' | sort -CloudApiEvents

ask_ai_prompts: Which non-browser process is using trusted cloud services in a suspicious way?
Correlate this cloud-service activity with file discovery, staging, or outbound byte-volume evidence.

references.name: Black Hills InfoSec FreeLabFriday Labs - Cloud-Based Services as Exfil

falsepositives: Software update services using cloud APIs
